title: How to Use Bulk Actions in GoHighLevel — Sales Pipeline
description: Learn how to use bulk actions in GoHighLevel's Opportunities feature to manage your sales pipeline 10x faster. Step-by-step guide for agencies and sales teams.
html_content:
Managing a sales pipeline with dozens—or hundreds—of opportunities can quickly become overwhelming. As a digital marketing agency owner or sales manager, you're constantly juggling prospect statuses, updating deal values, and moving cards across your Kanban board. What if you could handle all of that in seconds instead of hours?

That's where bulk actions in GoHighLevel's Opportunities feature come in. This powerful functionality lets you select multiple opportunity cards at once and perform batch updates—from changing pipeline stages to adding tags, updating deal values, and even deleting stale prospects. In this guide, I'll walk you through exactly how to use bulk actions to streamline your sales pipeline and close deals faster.

Ready to transform how you manage your pipeline? Start with a free 30-day GoHighLevel trial (double the standard trial) and see how bulk actions can cut your admin time in half.

📋 In This Guide

  • What Are Bulk Actions and Why They Matter for Your Sales Pipeline
  • How to Select Multiple Opportunity Cards in GoHighLevel
  • Step-by-Step: Performing Bulk Actions on Your Opportunities
  • Common Bulk Action Workflows for Sales Teams
  • Best Practices for Managing Your Sales Pipeline Efficiently

What Are Bulk Actions and Why They Matter for Your Sales Pipeline

Bulk actions are a batch processing feature in GoHighLevel that let you apply changes to multiple opportunity records simultaneously. Instead of clicking into each deal card individually and updating information one by one, you select the opportunities you want to modify and apply updates to all of them at once.

For sales teams and agencies, this capability is mission-critical. Consider this scenario: you've just closed a campaign that generated 50 new leads, and you need to move them all from "Lead" stage to "Qualified Prospect." Without bulk actions, that's 50 individual clicks. With bulk actions? One selection and one click.

Key benefits of using bulk actions include:

  • Speed: Update dozens of opportunities in under a minute
  • Consistency: Ensure all records are updated with the same information
  • Reduced errors: Less manual entry means fewer mistakes
  • Better focus: Spend less time on admin, more time on closing deals
  • Scalability: Manage larger pipelines without proportional time increases

How to Select Multiple Opportunity Cards in GoHighLevel

The first step to using bulk actions is knowing how to properly select opportunities from your Kanban board. GoHighLevel makes this straightforward, but there are a few selection methods you should know.

Individual Selection Method:

Start by navigating to your Opportunities section in GoHighLevel. You'll see your Kanban view with multiple pipeline stages displayed as columns. Each opportunity appears as a card within its respective stage.

To select individual opportunities, look for the checkbox on each card (typically in the top-left or top-right corner). Click the checkbox next to any opportunity you want to include in your bulk action. You can select as many as you need—there's no limit.

Select All on Current View:

If you want to select all visible opportunities on the current Kanban board, look for a "Select All" checkbox, usually located in the header area. Clicking this will automatically check all opportunities visible in your current view. This is particularly useful when you want to perform an action on all deals in a specific pipeline stage or filtered view.

💡 Pro Tip

Use GoHighLevel's filtering options before selecting all opportunities. Filter by pipeline stage, date created, or deal value to target a specific subset of deals without accidentally modifying unrelated opportunities.

Step-by-Step: Performing Bulk Actions on Your Opportunities

Once you've selected your opportunities, the bulk actions menu becomes available. Here's the exact process:

Step 1: Select Your Opportunities

Using the methods above, check the boxes for all opportunities you want to modify. You'll see a counter appear showing how many opportunities are selected (e.g., "12 selected").

Step 2: Access the Bulk Actions Menu

After selecting at least one opportunity, a bulk actions toolbar or dropdown menu appears. This is typically located at the top of your Kanban board or in a floating action bar. Click on it to reveal your available bulk action options.

Step 3: Choose Your Action

GoHighLevel offers several bulk action options for opportunities:

  • Update Pipeline Stage: Move all selected opportunities to a different stage in your pipeline
  • Add Tags: Apply tags to all selected opportunities for organization and filtering
  • Update Deal Value: Modify the monetary value of multiple deals at once
  • Assign to User: Reassign opportunities to different team members in bulk
  • Update Status: Change the status of multiple opportunities simultaneously
  • Delete: Remove multiple stale or duplicate opportunities from your pipeline
  • Add to Workflow: Enroll multiple opportunities into automated sequences

Step 4: Confirm and Apply

Select your desired action and fill in any required fields (e.g., which stage to move them to, what tag to add). Review your selection, then confirm. The system will process the action across all selected opportunities instantly.

Common Bulk Action Workflows for Sales Teams

Understanding common workflows will help you leverage bulk actions strategically in your business. Here are the most effective patterns I've seen agencies and sales teams use:

Workflow #1: Fresh Lead Intake Processing

When you receive a batch of new leads from a campaign or lead magnet, use bulk actions to tag them with the source (e.g., "Webinar Lead") and automatically move them to your "Lead" stage. This standardizes your intake process and ensures consistency across all new prospects.

Workflow #2: Weekly Pipeline Cleanup

Every Friday, review opportunities that haven't been touched in 30+ days. Select those stale deals, tag them "Follow-up Needed," and assign them to your sales team for outreach. You could also move them to a "Re-engagement" stage to trigger automated SMS or email reminders.

Workflow #3: Batch Stage Progression

When prospects complete a qualification call, move them from "Qualified" to "Proposal Sent" in bulk. This is especially powerful if you use a smart list filtered by a custom field or tag that indicates they've completed that step.

Workflow #4: Seasonal Deal Cleanup

At month-end or quarter-end, identify lost or closed deals and remove them from your active pipeline. Select all "Closed Lost" opportunities and delete them (or move them to an archive stage if your process requires historical data).

Workflow #5: Team Reassignment

When a team member leaves or you're rebalancing workload, use bulk actions to reassign all their opportunities to another rep. Filter by assigned user, select all, then reassign in one action.

Best Practices for Managing Your Sales Pipeline Efficiently

Bulk actions are powerful, but they're most effective when paired with intentional pipeline management practices.

Create a Consistent Tagging System

Before you start using bulk actions, establish a tagging standard. Use tags for source ("Google Ads," "Referral"), stage intent ("Hot Lead," "Nurture"), or action items ("Call scheduled," "Waiting for response"). This makes it easier to filter and select the right opportunities for bulk actions.

Use Filters Before Bulk Selecting

GoHighLevel's filtering options are your best friend. Filter by stage, assigned user, deal value, or date range before selecting all. This prevents accidental bulk updates on opportunities you didn't intend to modify.

Batch Your Updates by Day

Rather than making random updates throughout the day, batch your bulk actions into a scheduled time block. This keeps your pipeline data fresher and gives you a comprehensive view of daily progress.

Audit Before Deleting

When using the delete bulk action, double-check your selection. Consider moving to an "Archive" stage first rather than permanently deleting, so you retain historical data for reporting and analysis.

Automate with Workflows

For recurring bulk actions (like adding prospects to nurture sequences), use GoHighLevel's workflow automation instead of manual bulk actions. This ensures consistent execution without human error.
